entries.name: Next.js 14
entries.description: React framework with App Router, Server Components, and edge runtime

install.linux: curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | sudo -E bash - && sudo apt-get install -y nodejs

setup_steps: Start the dev server: npm run dev

entries.name: Go + chi
entries.description: Idiomatic Go HTTP server using the chi router and stdlib net/http

entries.name: PostgreSQL 16
entries.description: Open-source relational database with JSONB, full-text search, and partitioning
setup_steps: Start the server: brew services start postgresql@16  (macOS)

entries.name: Upstash Redis
entries.description: Serverless Redis with HTTP/REST access and global replication

account.note: Free tier includes 10,000 commands/day. No credit card required.

entries.boilerplate_snippet:
import { Redis } from '@upstash/redis';
export const redis = Redis.fromEnv();
setup_steps: Create a Redis database at console.upstash.com

entries.name: Clerk
entries.description: Drop-in authentication with session, user, and org management

account.note: Free tier: 10,000 monthly active users. Paid plans for advanced features.

setup_steps: Create an application in the Clerk dashboard

entries.name: Stripe
entries.description: Payments, subscriptions, and billing with webhook-driven event model

install.linux: curl -s https://packages.stripe.dev/api/security/keypair/stripe-cli-gpg/public | gpg --dearmor | sudo tee /usr/share/keyrings/stripe.gpg && echo 'deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/stripe.gpg] https://packages.stripe.dev/stripe-cli-debian-local stable main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/stripe.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y stripe
install.windows: scoop bucket add stripe https://github.com/stripe/scoop-stripe-cli.git && scoop install stripe

account.note: Stripe requires business details to activate live mode. Test mode works without verification.

auth.note: Opens your browser to authorize the Stripe CLI against your account.

entries.boilerplate_snippet:
import Stripe from 'stripe';
export const stripe = new Stripe(process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, { apiVersion: '2024-06-20' });
setup_steps: Install the Stripe CLI and run `stripe login`

entries.name: Docker
entries.description: Containerize services for local development and deployment
entries.boilerplate_snippet:
FROM golang:1.22-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /src
COPY . .
RUN go build -o /out/app ./...
FROM gcr.io/distroless/base-debian12
COPY --from=build /out/app /app
ENTRYPOINT ["/app"]
setup_steps: Start Docker Desktop (macOS/Windows) or the dockerd service (Linux)

entries.name: Fly.io
entries.description: Edge deployment platform with global anycast routing

account.note: Free tier available. No credit card required for hobby projects.

auth.note: This will open your browser to complete authentication.

entries.boilerplate_snippet:
app = "{{.AppName}}"
primary_region = "iad"
[http_service]
  internal_port = 8080
  force_https = true
setup_steps: Run `flyctl launch --no-deploy` to initialise your app

entries.name: Vercel
entries.description: Frontend platform with global edge network and preview deploys per PR

account.note: Free hobby tier available. Pro plans for teams and higher limits.

auth.note: Opens your browser to authorize the Vercel CLI.

setup_steps: Run `vercel` and link the project to your Vercel account

entries.name: Supabase
entries.description: Hosted Postgres with auth, realtime, storage, and edge functions

install.windows: scoop bucket add supabase https://github.com/supabase/scoop-bucket.git && scoop install supabase

auth.note: Opens your browser to generate an access token.

entries.name: PlanetScale
entries.description: Serverless MySQL with branch-per-PR workflow and non-blocking schema changes

install.windows: scoop bucket add pscale https://github.com/planetscale/scoop-bucket.git && scoop install pscale

account.note: Scaler plan required for new accounts (no free tier).

auth.note: Opens your browser to confirm the CLI login.

entries.boilerplate_snippet: DATABASE_URL="mysql://user:pass@host/{{.AppName}}?ssl={\"rejectUnauthorized\":true}"
setup_steps: Create a database: pscale database create {{.AppName}}
Create a branch: pscale branch create {{.AppName}} main
Get a connection string: pscale password create {{.AppName}} main app

entries.name: Prisma
entries.description: Type-safe database ORM with migrations, generated client, and Studio
setup_steps: Edit schema.prisma, then run: npx prisma migrate dev --name init

entries.name: Drizzle
entries.description: TypeScript-first SQL query builder and lightweight ORM

install.macos: npm install drizzle-orm && npm install -D drizzle-kit
install.linux: npm install drizzle-orm && npm install -D drizzle-kit
install.windows: npm install drizzle-orm && npm install -D drizzle-kit

setup_steps: Create drizzle.config.ts pointing at your schema

entries.name: tRPC
entries.description: End-to-end type-safe RPC between server and client without codegen

entries.name: Turborepo
entries.description: Monorepo build system with remote caching and parallel task execution

account.note: Remote caching (optional) is served by Vercel — sign in with `turbo login` to enable.

setup_steps: Create turbo.json at the repo root

entries.name: GitHub Actions
entries.description: CI/CD pipelines triggered by repo events with hosted runners

account.note: A GitHub account is needed to push the repo and trigger Actions.

auth.note: Opens your browser (or prints a device code) to authorize the CLI.

entries.boilerplate_snippet:
name: ci
on: { push: { branches: [main] }, pull_request: {} }
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with: { node-version: '20', cache: 'npm' }
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
setup_steps: Add secrets in Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions

entries.name: Railway
entries.description: Infrastructure platform that deploys apps + databases from your git repo

account.note: Trial includes $5 of usage; pay-as-you-go afterward.

auth.note: Opens your browser to authorize the Railway CLI.

entries.boilerplate_snippet:
# railway.toml
[build]
builder = "NIXPACKS"

[deploy]
startCommand = "./app"
restartPolicyType = "ON_FAILURE"

entries.name: Neon
entries.description: Serverless Postgres with branching, autoscaling, and scale-to-zero

account.note: Free tier: 1 project, 0.5 GB storage, always-on.

entries.boilerplate_snippet: DATABASE_URL="postgres://user:pass@ep-xxx.neon.tech/{{.AppName}}?sslmode=require"
setup_steps: Create a project: neonctl projects create --name {{.AppName}}
Grab the connection string: neonctl connection-string

entries.name: Resend
entries.description: Transactional email API for developers with React email templates

account.note: Free tier: 3,000 emails/month and 100/day. Domain verification required to send.

entries.boilerplate_snippet:
import { Resend } from 'resend';
export const resend = new Resend(process.env.RESEND_API_KEY);
setup_steps: Create an API key in the Resend dashboard
Verify your sending domain under Domains

entries.name: AWS S3
entries.description: Object storage for uploads, static assets, and backups

account.note: AWS requires a credit card. Free tier includes 5 GB of S3 storage for 12 months.

auth.note: Interactive prompt for your Access Key ID, Secret Access Key, region, and output format.

entries.boilerplate_snippet:
import { S3Client } from '@aws-sdk/client-s3';
export const s3 = new S3Client({ region: process.env.AWS_REGION });
setup_steps: Create an S3 bucket: aws s3 mb s3://{{.AppName}}-assets
Set a bucket policy for read or presigned-URL access
